Name: spectral_pac_bayes
Title: Spectral PAC-Bayes Framework for Low-Latency Inference with Adaptive Mixed-Precision Quantization
Short Hypothesis: By integrating spectral stability bounds with PAC-Bayes frameworks and adaptive mixed-precision quantization, we can develop a novel framework that achieves low-latency inference while maintaining model quality, thereby addressing the challenges of efficient deployment of large language models and vision transformers.
Related Work: Our proposal builds upon recent works such as SNIP, AMP-ViT, and EMQ, which have introduced adaptive mixed-precision training and quantization frameworks for large language models and vision transformers. However, our approach uniquely combines spectral stability bounds with PAC-Bayes frameworks to provide a more robust and efficient solution for low-latency inference. Unlike existing methods, our framework will utilize a fine-grained adaptive mixed-precision quantization approach, guided by spectral stability bounds and PAC-Bayes frameworks, to minimize quality loss while meeting efficiency targets.
Abstract: This proposal introduces a novel spectral PAC-Bayes framework for low-latency inference with adaptive mixed-precision quantization. By integrating spectral stability bounds with PAC-Bayes frameworks, our approach provides a robust and efficient solution for the deployment of large language models and vision transformers. Our framework utilizes a fine-grained adaptive mixed-precision quantization approach, guided by spectral stability bounds and PAC-Bayes frameworks, to minimize quality loss while meeting efficiency targets. We will conduct experiments on various models, including LLMs and ViTs, to demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach in achieving low-latency inference while maintaining model quality.
Experiments: We will conduct experiments on various models, including LLMs and ViTs, to evaluate the performance of our spectral PAC-Bayes framework. Our evaluation metrics will include latency, model quality, and computational overhead. We will compare our approach with state-of-the-art baselines, such as SNIP and AMP-ViT, to demonstrate its effectiveness in achieving low-latency inference while maintaining model quality.
Risk Factors and Limitations: One potential risk factor is the complexity of integrating spectral stability bounds with PAC-Bayes frameworks, which may require significant computational resources and expertise. Another limitation is the potential trade-off between model quality and latency, which may require careful tuning of hyperparameters to achieve optimal results.
